name: History and Sexuality 2
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 211, HIST 215 or HIST 221 recommended.
description: 1700 to the present, with a particular focus on Europe and North America. Possible topics include: patterns of fertility and sexual practice; prostitution; religion and sexuality; the medical and legal construction of sexualities; the rise of sexology; gay liberation movements; queer politics.

name: Greece: From Ottoman to the European Union
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 214, HIST 215, HIST 226 or HIST 262 recommended.
description: Examines the emergence of a modern nation state in the Balkans out of the Ottoman empire and its evolution until its present status as a member state of the European Union. A story of Greece and Greeks within the broader regional and global context.

name: Science and the Enlightenment
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 214, HIST 215 or HIST 238 recommended.
description: Explores the relationship between the natural sciences and the eighteenth-century Enlightenment. Examination of works in post-Newtonian science as well as their broader cultural meaning, the history of material practices, the origins of social science, and the role of geography and international context beyond Western Europe.

name: Themes in U.S. History since 1865
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 211 or HIST 221 recommended.
description: Aspects of American history from the gilded Age through the Cold War era.

name: African Diaspora Post-Emancipation
subject: HIST
description:
Exploration of the ways that diasporic Africans in the Atlantic World imagined freedom after European empires nominally abolished chattel enslavement in the mid-nineteenth century, including resistance, migrations, mass social movements, trans-Atlantic organizing, as well as imperialism, colonialism, and neo-colonialism from the
 
nineteenth century to the contemporary period.

name: History of Montreal
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 202 or HIST 203 recommended.
description: The history of Montreal from its beginnings to the present day. Montreal's economic, social, cultural and political role within the French and British empires, North America, Canada, and Quebec; the city's  linguistic and ethnic diversity.

name: Women in Europe 1700-2000
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 214, HIST 215 or HIST 226 recommended.
description: An overview of the history of women in modern continental Europe, focusing on women's changing roles in the family and society at large, in the context of work, family life, education, and culture, and the changing notions of citizenship, femininity, and masculinity.

name: Medicine in the Medieval West
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 212, HIST 214 or HIST 249 recommended.
description: The history of ideas about the human body, disease and therapeutics and the diverse practices of medicine in western Europe in the Middle Ages (ca. AD 300-1500), with particular attention to their social, intellectual, cultural and religious context.

name: Cultural Diversity in Canada
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 202 or HIST 203 recommended.
description: Selected topics in cultural diversity, society and the state in 19th, 20th and/or 21st centuries will be explored through discussion of primary and secondary historical sources.

name: China's Middle Empires
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 208 recommended.
description: Developments of China's middle empires, ca. 600-1300 CE.  Studies changing international relations, rapid commercialization, religious developments, the rise of the civil service examination system, and ensuing social change.

name: Latin America since 1825
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 210 or HIST 213 recommended
description: Themes in the political, economic, and social development of Latin America since the wars of independence.

name: Topics in Canadian Regional History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 202 or HIST 203 recommended.
description: Topics in Canadian regional history. Topics will vary by year.

name: Topics in Canadian Regional History
subject: HIST
description: Topics in Canadian regional history. Topics will vary by year.

name: Byzantine History and Historiography
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 205, HIST 212 or HIST 275 recommended.
description: Examines the political, social, cultural, and economic aspects of the 1000-year story of the Eastern Roman Empire, also known as the Byzantine empire, as well as its posterity.

name: Canada 1870-1914
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 202 and HIST 203 recommended.
description: This course will examine social, economic, political and cultural aspects of Canadian society between 1870 and 1914. Topics covered will include aboriginal peoples, European settlement of the West, provincial rights, the national policy, social reform movements, industrialization, immigration and the rise of cities.

name: Canada 1914-1945
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 202 and HIST 203 recommended.
description: This course will examine Canada and Canadian society between 1914 and 1945. It will focus on the social, political, economic and cultural impact of the two World Wars and the economic crisis of the 1930s. Among the topics will be Canadian external relations, political and social protest, popular culture, demographic changes and prohibition.

name: China's Early Empires
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 208 recommended.
description: Considers China's early imperial period, from ca. 300 BCE to ca. 100 CE, including changing political, social, and economic institutions, court and intellectual life, population management, foreign relations, urban transformations, and textual and material culture.  Introduction to the major historiographical sources covering the period, and to newly excavated texts, artifacts, and sites.

name: Themes in Latin American History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 210 or HIST 213 recommended.
description: Exploration of a specific topic in the history of Latin America and the Caribbean, 1492 to the present.

name: Canada since 1945
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 203 recommended.
description: Elements of Canada's political, social, economic, and cultural history since World War II. Topics will include constitutional questions, gender and class issues, the role of the state, regionalism, consumer society, the Quiet Revolution, and nationalism in Canada.

name: Greek History: Classical Period
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 205, CLAS 201 or CLAS 203 recommended.
description: The Classical period of Greek history, from the end of the Persian wars to the death of Alexandra the Great (479-323 BC).

name: Greek History: Early Greece
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 205, CLAS 201 or CLAS 203 recommended.
description: Historical study of the period from the Mycenean Age to the end of the Archaic Age.

name: Topics: Canadian Political History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 202 or HIST 203 recommended.
description: Explores a topic related to the political history of Canada.

name: American Civil Rights 1877-1940
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 211 or HIST 221 recommended.
description: The social, economic, political, and constitutional history of citizenship and civil rights in the United States from the end of Reconstruction through the 1930s. Emphasis on segregation and disfranchisement; immigration restrictions, americanization and national identities; civil rights movements and organizations; women's suffrage; voting rights and representation.

name: Themes in Medieval History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 212 or HIST 214 recommended.
description: Explores various aspects of the Middle Ages through thematic approaches. Examines specific issues such as gender, intercultural relations, trade and commerce, diplomatic history, urban development, or religious development in the medieval period, through a variety of sources, including narrative and archival documents, archaeological data, and material culture. Historical methodology in relation to the study of the Middle Ages is emphasized. Topics vary by year.

name: History of Sexuality in Canada
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 202 or HIST 203 recommended.
description: Sexuality as theory, culture, and historical experience in Canada from early contact period to the present. Chronological and thematic format. Topics include fertility and its control, prostitution and pornography, sexual repression and liberation in medicine and politics, queer theory and economies of pleasure.

name: Rome: Republic to Empire
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 205, HIST 275, HIST 207 or CLAS 201 recommended.
description: Roman history from the Gracchi to the death of the emperor Nero, 133 BCE to 68 CE. The collapse of the Roman republic, the reorganization of the Roman state under Augustus, and the Roman empire and society under the early emperors.

name: Fall of the Roman Empire
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 205, HIST 275, HIST 212 or CLAS 201 recommended.
description: Transformation of the Roman world, 1st-7th CE. Considers various perspectives and narratives of the decline and fall of the Roman Empire based on historical and material evidence.

name: The United States, 1940-1965
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: any course in U.S. history or consent of instructor
description: Major events in politics and international affairs, culture and society, and the economy in the U.S. during and after World War II. Topics include: The War and American society; the first years of the Cold War; economic prosperity and social change; the civil rights movement; Vietnam to 1965.

name:
The Medieval
 Mediterranean
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 212, HIST 214 or permission of instructor
description: Thematic history of the Mediterranean during the Medieval period, covering elements of Latin, Byzantine and Islamic civilizations and their interactions.

name: Colonial Africa
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 200, HIST 201, HIST 206 or AFRI 200 recommended.
description: An overview of the history of foreign intervention and anticolonial resistance in 19th and 20th century Africa. Topics include: theories of colonialism, the scramble for Africa, colonialism and disease, indirect rule, labour, nationalism and resistance, and changing gender roles.

name: History of South Africa
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 200, HIST 201, HIST 206 or AFRI 200 recommended.
description: History of South Africa from precolonial times to the present. Topics include: precolonial societies; British and Dutch colonialism; slavery in colonial South Africa; the Zulu kingdom; mining capitalism; the Boer War; Afrikaner nationalism; apartheid; the anti-apartheid struggle; music, religion, and art; challenges of the post-apartheid state.

name: Eighteenth-Century Britain
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 215 or permission of instructor.
description: Cultural, intellectual, political, economic and social history of Britain and Ireland in the eighteenth century; the era of the creation of the United Kingdom and the rise of a great commercial and imperial power.

name: Nineteenth-Century Britain
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 215 or permission of instructor
description: Cultural, intellectual, political, economic and social history of Britain and Ireland in an era of unprecedented economic and cultural change as the United Kingdom became the world's first industrial nation and leading imperial power.

name: Twentieth-Century Britain
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 215 or permission of instructor
description: From a range of perspectives, including cultural, intellectual, political, economic and social history, this course examines Britain from the height of its power, through two world wars, the building of a welfare state, the dissolution of Empire and entry into Europe, to the start of the 21st century. consensus, decolonisation, immigration, culture and society, Northern Ireland, Scottish and Welsh nationalism, Thatcherism, the European Union.

name: The First World War
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 203, HIST 215, HIST 216, HIST 221 or HIST 226 recommended.
description: A world-wide political, social, economic, cultural and military survey, from the origins of the Great War to the Treaty of Versailles.

name: The Second World War
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 203 , HIST 215, HIST 216, HIST 221 or HIST 226 recommended.
description: A world-wide political, social, economic, cultural and military survey, from the Treaty of Versailles to the first years of the Cold War.

name: Topics: African Country Survey
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 200, HIST 201, HIST 206 or AFRI 200 recommended.
description: In depth survey of a single African country (other than South Africa), including the pre-colonial history of the region, colonialism, and post-colonial economic, cultural and political history.

name: Topics: African Country Survey
subject: HIST
description: In depth survey of a single African country (other than South Africa), including the pre-colonial history of the region, colonialism, and post-colonial economic, cultural and political history.

name: Eighteenth-Century France
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 214 or HIST 215 or HIST 225 or permission of Instructor
description: The political, social, and cultural history of France, from the accession of Louis XV (1715) to the rise of Napoleon (1799), including the French Revolution.

name: Rise of Rome
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 205, HIST 275, HIST 207 or CLAS 201 recommended.
description: Rome's rise from city-state to world power, 338 - 133 BCE, the nature Roman conquest, and the impact of empire on Roman society.

name: The United States since 1965
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 211 or HIST 221 recommended.
description: Major events in politics and international affairs, culture and society, and economy in the U.S. since 1965. Topics include: social and political upheaval 1965 - 1975; Vietnam to 1975; conservative politics; Nixon and Watergate; economic change in the 1970s and 1980s; presidential leadership from Carter on.

name: Civil War and Reconstruction
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 211 or HIST 221 recommended.
description: The causes of the American Civil War; the social, economic, political and military forces that shaped the conflict, attempts to restructure race relations, Southern and American societies after the war.

name: British Revolutions 1567-1660
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 214 or HIST 250 recommended.
description: English, Scottish and Irish history during the late sixteenth and early seventeenth centuries, with a particular focus on the origins and consequences of the regicides of Mary Queen of Scots in 1587 and King Charles I in 1649.

name: Canada: Ethnicity, Migration
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 203 recommended
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken HIST 423
description: Immigration, ethnicity and race in Canada in the nineteenth and twentieth centuries. Topics will include the migration process, government policy and legislation, urban and rural migration, acculturation, nativism and multiculturalism.

name: Topics in Italian History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 214 or HIST 212 recommended.
description: Topics in Italian history.  Topic varies by year.

name:
History and
 Historiography
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: 6 credits of History
restrictions: History Honours or Joint Honours Program students only, unless permission of instructor.
description: The nature and functions of history; changing conceptions of time and of the past; approaches to historical evidence; methods of reconstructing the past; how the discipline of history has changed over time.

name: Ancient Greece, Rome and China
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: ARLE 101 or HIST 205 or HIST 208 or permission of instructor
description: A comparative analysis of the political cultures of ancient Greece, Rome and China, c. 500 BCE to 500 CE, exploring societal distinctions through topics such as the role of historical traditions, power configurations, public oratory, elite representation, funerary rites and political spaces.

name: Topics: Medieval Culture and Society
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 214 or HIST 380 or consent of instructor
description: Selected topics in the intellectual and cultural history of the Middle Ages. Emphasis on modern critical approaches to medieval culture, including literature, the supernatural, religious experience.

name: Topics:Medieval Culture & Soc
subject: HIST
description: Selected topics in the intellectual and cultural history of the Middle Ages. Emphasis on modern critical approaches to medieval culture, including literature, the supernatural, religious experience.

name: The History of Confucius
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: HIST 208 or HIST 218 or EAST 211 or permission of the instructor
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken EAST 490.
description: Confucius is inseparable from the idea of China or even East Asia. This course will unpack the manifold legacies of Confucius and his classics in pre-imperial, imperial, and modern China.

name: Topics in Intellectual History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: HIST 320 or permission of the instructor.
description: In depth analysis of a selected subject or topic in intellectual history and/or the history of ideas.

name: Topics: Russian History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: A prior course in Russian or European history
description: Examination of a selected topic in Russian history from the reign of Peter the Great to the present time.

name: Topics in Ancient History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: 3 credits in Ancient history at the 300-level or permission of instructor.
description: An in-depth look at various topics in ancient history.

name: Selected Topics in Indigenous History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 223
description: Selected topics in Indigenous history.

name: Topics in Latin American History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: HIST 210, HIST 309, HIST 360, HIST 366 or permission of instructor.
description: In-depth discussion and research on a circumscribed topic in the history of Latin America and the Caribbean, 1492 to the present.

name: Topics in Latin American History
subject: HIST
description: In-depth discussion and research on a circumscribed topic in the history of Latin America and the Caribbean, 1492 to the present.

name: Topics in Latin American History
subject: HIST
description: In-depth discussion and research on a circumscribed topic in the history of Latin America and the Caribbean, 1492 to the present.

name: Topics in History of Science
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: A prior course on history of science or HPSC 300 or permission of Instructor.
restrictions: Not open to U0 or U1 students
description: Specific theme in the history of science, such as scientific instruments, experimental practices, uses of the body, knowledge and museums, scientific institutions, or science and empire.

name: Topics in African History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: A previous course in African history or permission of instructor.
description: In depth examination of a specific topic or individual in African or African diasporic history.

name: Women and Gender in Modern Britain
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 215 or a course in British history or permission of instructor
description: Women and gender in modern Britain (1850 on). Topics include early feminist political agitation, including the suffrage movement; working-class women; changing notions of gender, sexuality and women's role; women and empire.

name: Post-Soviet History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 216 or HIST 326 or permission of the instructor
description: Analyzing developments since 1991 across the former Soviet Union, with a particular but not exclusive focus on Russia, and looking at the region's political, economic, and social transformations.

name: British and Irish Nationalisms
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: A course in modern British history or permission of instructor.
description: The history of Irish, Scottish, Welsh and English nationalisms in the British context from 1688 to the present.

name: Topics: Atlantic World
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: any two of the following: HIST 200, HIST 202, HIST 211, HIST 214, HIST 309 or permission of instructor.
restrictions: Enrolment limit 25.
description: Exploration of a specific theme in Atlantic history, 1500 to 1850.

name: Gender and Sexuality in Modern China
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: A 300-level course in the History of China or Gender/Sexuality or permission of instructor.
description: The history of gender and sexuality in modern China. Topics include Chinese femininities and Chinese masculinities, theories of sexuality, and changing conceptions of gender identity under Confucianism, Western Imperialism, and socialism.

name: Topics in Early Modern Europe
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: a course in Early Modern Europe
description: Varying subjects of topical interest regarding early-modern Europe.

name: Topics: Migration and Ethnicity
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 397 or permission of instructor
description: The study of various topics and themes in the area of migration, ethnicity and race in Canada. Topics vary from year to year.

name: Topics: Migration and Ethnicity
subject: HIST
description: The study of various topics and themes in the area of migration, ethnicity and race in Canada. Topics vary from year to year.

name: Gender, Sexuality and Medicine
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: A 300-level History course in gender, sexuality or medicine or permission of instructor.
description: Gender, sexuality, and medicine since the colonial era, with a focus on North American experience. Topics will include reproductive medicine (puberty, childbirth, fertility control, menopause), changing perceptions of men's and women's health needs and risks, and ideas about sexual behaviour and identity.

name: Global Food History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: one course in History or permission of instructor.
description: A history of food and drink in global history. Topics include: feasts and famines; the exchange of foodstuffs and drinking habits between cultures, especially the early modern 'Columbian exchange'; the history of civilizing processes (e.g, table manners); the origins of the restaurant and the making of modern food systems.

name: Topics: British Cultural History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 215 or a course in British history or permission of instructor
description: Selected topics in intellectual and cultural history of Britain and Ireland, focusing on discussion of primary texts.

name: The Hasidic Movement
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 307 or a course in East-European history or consent of instructor
description: A historical examination of the history of the Hasidic Movement from its beginnings in 18th-century Poland to the present. Although emphasis will be placed on the social history of the movement, doctrinal developments will be examined as well.

name: Topics: History of the Book
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: A 300-level course in history or permission of instructor.
description: The theory and the practice of using books, manuscripts, periodicals as well as other communicative media. Topics may include literacy and orality; the print revolution; censorship; readers and reading practices; newspapers and journalism; the origins of scientific persuasion and intellectual property rights.

name: Topics: Gender/Feminist Histories
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): A 200-level course in History or permission of instructor.
description: An intensive study of selected aspects related to the history of gender, feminism, and sexuality.

name: Topics: Gender/Feminist Histories
subject: HIST
description: An intensive study of selected aspects related to the history of gender, feminism, and sexuality.

name: Topics in Modern Medicine
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: HIST 249 (or HIST 349 prior to Winter 2006) or permission of the instructor.
description: Selected topics in the history of medicine in the 19th, 20th and/or 21st centuries will be explored through discussion of primary and secondary historical sources.

name: Topics in U.S. History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: By permission of instructor.
description: Various topics in United States history.

name: Topics in Labour History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 202 and HIST 203 or consent of the instructor
description: Exploration of selected themes in labour and working-class history from diverse geographical and transnational perspectives.

name: British Queer History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: HIST 215 or a course in British History or permission of instructor.
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken HIST 426 in 200209.
description: An investigation of the changing historical construction of "deviant" and "normal" sexualities in Britain since 1700, and how queer women and men discovered ways of surviving and perhaps even flourishing in the face of persecution and hostility from the state, the churches and the medical profession.

name: British North America 1760-1867
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: An introductory course in history or consent or instructor
description: This course will study the social-cultural and political development of British North American colonies.

name: Topics in South Asian History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 310 or HIST 341 or permission of instructor.
description: In-depth discussion and research on a topic in the history of South Asia.

name: Topics: European History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: Permission of instructor.
description: An in-depth look at particular aspects of European history.

name: Topics: European History
subject: HIST
description: An in-depth look at particular aspects of European history.

name: Topics: European History
subject: HIST
description: An in-depth look at particular aspects of European history.

name: Topics: Issues in Canadian History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): A 300-level course in Canadian History or permission of instructor.
description: A discussion-based, advanced intensive study of selected aspects of Canadian history.

name: Topics: Issues in Canadian History
subject: HIST
description: A discussion-based, advanced intensive study of selected aspects of Canadian history.

name: Topics in Cold War History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 304 or other 300-level course relevant to the current topic of the course or permission on the instructor.
description: One large aspect of Cold War, either thematic or regional, will be explored.

name: Topics in Cold War History
subject: HIST
description: One large aspect of Cold War, either thematic or regional, will be explored.

name: History of Women in China
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: a previous course in Chinese history
description: This course examines the changing roles of women in traditional and modern China. Topics include political, social, and legal status, sexuality and medicine, religion and culture.

name: Fiction and History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: 6 credits at the 300 level in either history or literature
description: This course examines why and how books are classified as fiction or history. Topics include: social expectations and uses of literature; evidence and verification; the author as authority. Readings include history and fiction from various historical periods, and relevant scholarship.

name: Topics: Culture and Ritual in China
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 208 and HIST 218 and permission of instructor
description: An examination of selected aspects of the cultural and intellectual life of China. Topics vary from year to year, but include the history of popular religion, Chinese science and medicine, the esoteric arts including divination practices, law, and the influence of ideas in the production of Chinese culture.

name: Asian Diaspora: Chinese Overseas
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: One previous course in Chinese or Asian history or permission of instructor
description: The contexts and causes of Chinese emigration; historical patterns of migration; Overseas Chinese communities on five continents, with emphasis on Southeast Asia and North America; alienation and identity in Chinatown; relations between the Overseas Chinese and China.

name: Topics: Modern Japan
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 218 or permission of the instructor.
description: An intensive study of selected aspects related to the history of modern Japan.

name: Late Imperial China
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 208 or HIST 218
description: An introduction to the social and economic history of Late Imperial China, focusing on the Ming and early to mid Qing Dynasties (1368 - 1800), and current interpretations thereof. Was this a discrete period in Chinese history? If so, why.

name: Topics in Middle East History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): Any class on the history of the Middle East or permission of the instructor
description: Examination of a selected topic in the history of the modern Middle East from the late 19th century to the present.

name: Ancient History Methods
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: 3 credits at the 300-level in Ancient history or permission of the instructor.
description: Different methods and strategies employed by Ancient historians, including numismatics, epigraphy, and papyrology.

name: Topics in Pre-Modern Medicine
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: HIST 214 or HIST 249 and a 300-level course in History or permission of instructor.
description: The history of the evolution of ideas about the human body, disease and therapeutics and the diverse practices of medicine prior to the advent of modern clinical and laboratory medicine in the 19th c., with particular attention to social, political, cultural and religious context.

name: Topics in Pre-Modern Medicine
subject: HIST
description: The history of the evolution of ideas about the human body, disease and therapeutics and the diverse practices of medicine prior to the advent of modern clinical and laboratory medicine in the 19th c., with particular attention to social, political, cultural and religious context.

name: History of Revolution in Europe
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 215 or permission of instructor
description:
The evolution of the concept and phenomenon of revolution from the 1640s in England to 1989 in eastern Europe. How the experiences of 1789, 1848, and
1917 changed the theory and practice of revolution.

name: Marxist Theory
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: A 200-level history course or permission of instructor.
description: Themes in Marxist theory and historiography, from the mid-19th century to the present moment. Topics will include the global intellectual history of Marxism in political and economic thought, diverse Marxisms around the world, contemporary developments in Marxist theory, historical analysis and historiography.

name: Topics in Medical History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 349 or HIST 356 or permission of instructor
description: This course explores different topics in medical history. Topics to be explored include the role of medicine from ancient to modern times.

name: Topics in Medical History
subject: HIST
description: This course explores different topics in medical history. Topics to be explored include the role of medicine from ancient to modern times.

name: Topics in Medical History
subject: HIST
description: This course explores different topics in medical history. Topics to be explored include the role of medicine from ancient to modern times.

name: Europe Since 1989
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): A 300-level course in European history or permission of instructor.
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken HIST 436 in Winter 2012 or Winter 2014.
description: Though the European continent has changed beyond recognition since 1989, historians are only just beginning to turn their attention to this most contemporary of European epochs. This course therefore provides students an opportunity to conduct ground-breaking research, informed by readings and seminar discussions on such topics as Thatcherism, the round tables and revolutions of 1989, war and genocide in Yugoslavia, European integration, the fall and rise of Russia, technological change, and related transformations of society and culture. The course provides training in methods appropriate for research on contemporary history, and though it does not neglect great powers, it is well suited to students interested in Europe’s small countries.

name: Alexander and Hellenistic World
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): 3 credits in Ancient history at the 300-level or permission of instructor.
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken HIST 407 in winter 2015 or winter 2017.
description:
The history and culture of the Hellenistic period, from the Age of Alexander to the rise of the successor kingdoms and their interactions with Rome; discusses the
development of a new sense of globality and its impact on the local horizon of cities across the Mediterranean and beyond.

name: History of European Education
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 215 or permission of instructor.
description: A history of school and schooling within the European context since the invention of print. Topics include: passage from private to public practices; modern statehood and education; school and social mobility; school and the civilizing mission; student mobility; universities and the specialization of academic disciplines; Europe and the globalization of the educational field.

name: Topics: Roman History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 205, HIST 275, HIST 375, HIST 376, HIST 391, or permission of instructor.
description: In-depth examination of a selected topic, theme, or period of ancient Roman history.

name: Topics: Roman History
subject: HIST
description: In-depth examination of a selected topic, theme, or period of ancient Roman history.

name: Pre-modern Chinese Law and Society
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: Any 300-level course in Chinese history or permission of the instructor.
description: The history of Chinese law and society from early pre-imperial to late imperial times. Themes include the philosophical basis of Chinese law; development of different forms of legislation; practice of pre-modern law; law and social and political change; military law; legal cases translated from primary sources.

name: Democracy and Society in India
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 341 or permission of instructor.
description: A specialized in-depth historical survey of issues and problems in the transformation of India towards democracy in the post-colonial era.

name: History of Bangladesh and Pakistan
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 341 or permission of instructor.
description: Examines social and political histories of Pakistan and Bangladesh from 1947 to present with attention to the roles of Islam, ethnicity and nationalism.

name: Global History of Coffee
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): Any 300-level history course or permission of instructor.
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken HIST 436 when topic was "Cafes and Coffeehouses".
description: The history of coffee and coffeehouses from the discovery of coffee in the late medieval Ottoman empire until the present day. Topics include the putative 'golden age' of coffeehouse culture in the early modern era, the transformations of the coffeehouse in the nineteenth and twentieth centuries, the development of a modern consumer society, and forms of public association and communication often described as the bourgeois public sphere.

name: Emotions History of Early Modern Europe
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: One course in European history or permission of the instructor.
description: Readings and research on the history of emotions in the early modern era (c.  1450 – c. 1800). Introduction to the topic with a focus on how historians have studied the emotional life of Europeans between the age of renaissance and reformation and the French Revolution, including selected studies of the medieval and modern eras.

name: Independent Research
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: Written permission from the instructor and the undergraduate program director.
restrictions: Open to History Major Concentration, Honours or Joint Honours students only. Not open to students who have taken HIST 413.
description:
Exceptionally, and under the direction of a member of staff, advanced and highly qualified students who have an extensive background in the proposed area
of study, may pursue this independent study.

name: Internship: History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: Permission of the departmental Internship Advisor.
restrictions: Open to U2 and U3 students with a minimum CGPA of 2.7, and permission of the departmental Internship Advisor.
description: Internship with an approved host institution or organization.

name: Topics in Modern European History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): Undergraduate students: HIST 215 or permission of instructor; MA students: a prior course in the history of modern Europe pertinent to the subject of the course or permission of the instructor.
description: Advanced topics in modern European history. Topics will vary by year. The course has a substantial research component.

name: Topics in Modern European History
subject: HIST
description: Advanced topics in modern European history. Topics will vary by year. The course has a substantial research component.

name: Topics: World History
subject: HIST
description: Selected topics in world history.

name: Topics: The British Empire
subject: HIST
description: Topics in the history of British formal and informal imperialism and the colonial encounter from the eighteenth to the twentieth centuries.

name: Topics: The British Empire
subject: HIST
description: Topics in the history of British formal and informal imperialism and the colonial encounter from the eighteenth to the twentieth centuries.

name: Topics in Early Modern History
subject: HIST
description: Readings and research on a selected topic in early modern history.

name: The Art of War in China
subject: HIST
description: A study of the historical development of military theory and practice from earliest times to 1911 from a variety of perspectives, technological, scientific, social, and cultural.

name: European Intellectual History
subject: HIST
description: Study of selected topics in European intellectual and/or cultural history.

name: Environmental History of Latin America (Field)
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: HISP 218 or HISP 210 or equivalent proficiency
description:
Human-nature interactions over different scales of time in Latin America (with an emphasis on neo-tropical environments) and the application of the historical perspective to contemporary environmental issues, including historiography and
methodology; cultures of environmental knowledge.

name: History of Emotions
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): At least one History course at the 400-level or above, or permission of instructor.
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken HIST 507 in fall 2016.
description: Readings and research on the history of emotions, with focus on how historians have studied the emotional life of the past. Particular attention to the various ways emotion has been conceived in the past and the historical methodologies used to study emotional experiences.

name: Topics in Chinese History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: A 300- or 400-level course in Chinese history or permission of instructor.
description: Exploration of a theme in Chinese history

name: Topics in Chinese History
subject: HIST
description: Exploration of a theme in Chinese history

name: Topics in Canadian History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): A 300-level course in Canadian history or permission of the instructor.
description: Advanced study of a selected topic in Canadian history.

name: Women, Work and Family in Global History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: A 300 or 400-level course in women's history or labour history or permission of instructor
restrictions: Restricted to students in History and Women's Studies
description: The shifting historical context of female labour and family in selected western and non-western countries; the interaction between labour and gender relations with special focus on women's experiences on the shop floor and in the family.

name: Women and War
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): A 300 or 400-levelcourse in women's history or permission of instructor
description: Examines the impact of war on individuals, families and societies. Studies the experiences of women and children in exile, mass persecutions, and punishments associated with social unrest, revolution or wars during twentieth century.

name: Topics: Indian Ocean World History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: Any 200 or 300 level history course or any course on Africa, Asia, global or labour studies or permission of instructor
description: This course studies pivotal topics in Indian Ocean World (IOW) history. Topics may include (i) the rise and development of the IOW global economy; (ii) The Swahili Civilisation and the IOW; (iii) Islam and the IOW; (iv) Imperialism in the IOW; (v) IOW Slavery and Slave Trades; (vi) History of Madagascar.

name: Indian Ocean World Slave Trade
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: HIST 200 or HIST 213 or permission of instructor.
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken HIST 467.
description: The origins, structure and impact of the Indian Ocean World slave trade from early times to the present day. Enslavement, the trading structure, slave functions, reactions to slavery, emancipation and 'slave' diaspora. Comparisons will be made to the Atlantic slave system.

name: U.S. Foreign Relations
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: one course in U.S. history or permission of instructor.
restrictions: Enrolment limit 25.
description: The history and historiography, approaches and interpretations, of American foreign relations from the pre-Revolutionary era to the present.

name: History and Biography
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: At least one HIST course at the 400 level or higher.
description: Biography is one of the oldest and most popular historical genres. This course examines the relationship between biographical life writing and historical analysis. Readings include exemplary works of both popular and scholarly biography as well as discussions of biographical research methods. The seminar is designed to prepare students to write their own biographical essay based on original research.

name: Ancient History: Seminar
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite (Undergraduate): 6 credits at the 300 or 400 level in Ancient history or permission of instructor.
restrictions: Honours students or advanced undergraduates who have permission of the instructor. Also open to graduate students.
description: Topics in ancient Mediterranean history, focusing on Greek and/or Roman society.

name: Ancient History: Seminar
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite (Undergraduate): 6 credits at the 300 or 400 level in Ancient history or permission of instructor. HIST 550D1.
restrictions: Honours students or advanced undergraduates who have permission of the instructor. Also open to graduate students.
description: Topics in ancient Mediterranean history, focusing on Greek and/or Roman society.

name: International Relations: Seminar
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): Permission of instructor.
restrictions: Restricted to Graduate students and Honours students or advanced students with permission of the instructor. Not open to students who have taken HIST 553.
description: Readings on and discussion of a theme in the history of international relations.

name: International Relations Seminar
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: Permission of instructor and HIST 552D1
restrictions: Restricted to Graduate students and Honours students or advanced students with permission of the instructor. Not open to students who have taken HIST 553.
description: Readings on and discussion of a theme in the history of international relations.

name: Seminar: Medieval History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 380 or HIST 401 or permission of instructor.
description: Readings and discussion on a selected topic in Medieval history.

name: Seminar: Medieval History
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: HIST 555D1
description: Readings and discussion on a selected topic in Medieval history.

name: Seminar: Colonial America
subject: HIST
restrictions: Restricted to Graduate Students and Honours students or advanced students with permission of instructor. Not open to students who have taken HIST 556 and HIST 557 with the same topic.
description: Readings in and discussion of a theme in the history of Colonial America.

name: Seminar: Colonial America
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): HIST 556D1
restrictions: Restricted to Graduate Students and Honours students or advanced students with permission of instructor. Not open to students who have taken HIST 556 and HIST 557 with the same topic.
description: Readings in and discussion of a theme in the history of Colonial America.

name: Modern Medicine: Seminar
subject: HIST
description: The emergence of scientific medicine, medical professionalization, the development of public health and the process of medical specialization since 1700.

name: Modern Medicine: Research
subject: HIST
description: Supervised design, research, writing, and discussion of a major research paper on a theme in the history of modern medicine since 1700.

name: World History: Seminar
subject: HIST
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: Permission of instructor.
restrictions: Restricted to Graduate Students and Honours students or advanced students with permission of instructor. Not open to students who have taken HIST 561.
description: Readings on and discussion of a theme in world history.

name: World History: Seminar

Themes will include transatlantic commerce and slavery, colonialism, and indigenous peoples, debates over citizenship and the Haitian Revolution.

subject: HIST

Provides historiographical grounding for graduate students in the area of Canadian history. Introduces a wide variety of writings on the topic, and examines how
historiographical approaches and themes have changed over time.

name: New France Research Seminar
subject: HIST
description: Introduction to the range of primary sources related to the history of New France (notarial, judicial, parish registers, government correspondence, cartography, travel writing, etc.) and instruction on relevant research methods.

name: Topics in Indigenous History
subject: HIST
description: Intensive study of selected topics in Indigenous history. Emphasizes methodologies and recent developments in the burgeoning field of Indigenous history.

name: Topics in European History
subject: HIST

name: Historical Methods

description: An examination of the major approaches to historical interpretation through the reading of important works of historical scholarship.

name: Empire and Colonialisms

description: Themes and approaches in economic history.

subject: HIST
description: An advanced introduction to the historical profession and to recent developments in historical scholarship.

name: Introduction to Labour-Management Relations
subject: INDR
description: An introduction to labour-management relations, the structure, function and government of labour unions, labour legislation, the collective bargaining process, and the public interest in industrial relations.

name: Comparative Employment Relations
subject: INDR
description: Comparison of employment relations in current socio-political and economic context of a variety of nations. Emphasis on historical and recent developments in labour-management relations, labour legislation, institutional structures, collective bargaining, contract coverage, and the role of multi-national corporations.

name: Globalization and Labour Policy
subject: INDR
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: INDR 294
description: Exploration of contemporary labour policies around the world in the context of globalization. Topics include labour market functioning, international trade, technological change, flexibility, employment standards, protective legislation, employment practices.

name: Labour Law
subject: INDR
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: INDR 294
description: Introduction to the basic concepts of labour law relevant to the practice of industrial relations. Historical development of labour law in certain social and legal systems and the culmination in the legislative enactments and jurisprudence of Canadian jurisdictions and certain comparative foreign models.

name: Collective Bargaining
subject: INDR
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: INDR 294
description: Principles of collective bargaining in Canada and abroad. Problem oriented. Mock collective bargaining sessions provide an opportunity for students to apply knowledge gained.

name: Internship in Industrial Relations
subject: INDR
restrictions: Open to U2 and U3 students after completing 30 credits of a 90 credits program or 45 credits of a 96-120 credit program, a minimum CGPA of 2.7, and permission of the departmental Internship Advisor. This course will normally not fulfill program requirements for seminar or 400-level courses. A letter from a supervisor at the institution must attest to successful completion of the student's tenure.
description: Internship with an approved host institution or organization.

name: First Year Seminar: Language and Mind
subject: LING
restrictions: Open only to newly admitted BA students in U0, who may take only one FYS.
description: This fast paced course introduces students to challenges faced by scientists who study how language is represented in the human brain.

name: Introduction to the Study of Language
subject: LING
description: General interest course; intended for students in all fields. Topics include: linguistic competence vs. performance, language and the brain, language acquisition, sociolinguistics, historical linguistics, language universals, pragmatics.

name: Introduction to Linguistics
subject: LING
description:
General introduction to linguistics, the scientific study of human language. Covers the core theoretical subfields of linguistics: phonetics, phonology, morphology, syntax, and semantics. Also provides background on other subfields including sociolinguistics, pragmatics, historical linguistics, linguistic variation, and language
acquisition.

name: Introduction to Speech Science
subject: LING
description: The course covers key concepts of speech science, including phonetics (acoustics, speech perception and production), fundamentals in the study of speech processing, speech development, and speech disorders, and introduces some basic methodologies of the field.

name: Introduction to Indigenous Languages
subject: LING
description: Explores the Indigenous languages of North America, including their histories, linguistic properties, cultural settings, and key social issues facing them in the 21st century. Many different aspects of North American languages will be discussed including sound systems, grammatical classification, expression of time and space, and specialized linguistic phenomena like polysynthesis and reduplication. Social contexts of language will include performance, speech games, language change, language endangerment, and government policies.

name: Meaning in Language
subject: LING
description: A hands-on introduction to the strategies that natural languages use to convey meaning. Requiring no previous background in linguistics, the course surveys fundamental properties of word and sentence meaning and their interdependence with context. It provides an overview of the grammatical mechanisms that languages employ to construct the literal meanings of sentences from word meanings, explores how meanings are anchored to real life situations, and analyzes how meanings are routinely enriched in context by language users to convey more than what is literally expressed.

name: Languages of the World
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 201
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken LING 215.
description: An introduction to the study of linguistics through a look at the world's languages. Topics covered will include linguistic universals and language diversity; language endangerment, death, and revitalization; and the relationship between language and thought.

name: Sociolinguistics 1
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 201.
description: A survey of language in its social context. The main focus will be on the influence of social factors like age, gender, social class and speech style on linguistic variation and change. Contact amongst languages (e.g. in Montreal) and the birth and death of languages will also be discussed.

name: Canadian English
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 200 or LING 201 or permission of the instructor
description: A diverse, interdisciplinary set of perspectives on the English language in Canada, including its status as one of many Canadian languages and as the expressive medium of English-Canadian culture, the history of English-speaking settlement in Canada, and linguistic analysis of its main distinguishing features, regional variation and changes in progress.

name: Phonetics
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LlNG 201
description: Transcription, identification, and production of speech sounds.  Introduction to the acoustic properties of speech sounds, acoustic analysis of speech, and auditory phonetics.

name: Phonology 1
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 330.
description: Introduction to phonological theory and analysis.

name: From Natural Language to Data Science
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: COMP 250 and MATH 133; or permission of the instructor.
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken or are taking COMP 345.
description: Introduction to language data science, including theoretical approaches and practical skills. Processing, searching, and querying text data; making sense of large corpora; modelling and interpreting psycholinguistic and historical language data; building models of sequences of words; computing similarity between languages; information retrieval and extraction; question answering; and ethics.

name: Linguistic Aspects of Bilingualism
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 200 or LING 201
description: Linguistic competence and performance in bilinguals: the organization of the bilingual's grammar. Syntactic constraints on code mixing: How many grammars are involved? Unidirectional and bidirectional grammatical interference. Structural distance between genetically related and unrelated languages and its effect on the organization of the bilingual's grammar.

name: Psycholinguistics
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: LING 201
description:
Introduction to experimental methods in linguistics, focusing on how linguistic
hypotheses can be tested experimentally, and how evidence from speech and
language processing can inform linguistic theory.

name: Language Acquisition 1
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 201.
description: A critical study of the application of linguistic theory and description to first and second language learning. Topics include: the acquisition of sounds, syntax and word meanings; acquisition strategies; properties of the input; theories of first and second language acquisition.

name: Introduction to Semantics
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: LING 201 and one of the following: PHIL 210, COMP 230, MATH 318
description: Introduction to the rudiments of semantics, focusing on those aspects of meaning that are invariant across contexts and the ways in which the meaning of a complex expression is determined by the meanings of its constituents.

name: Pragmatics 1
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): LING 201 or LING 260
description: A self-contained hands-on introduction to the study of how the conventional meaning of sentences relates to the context where they are uttered. Topics include the studyof meanings that are determined with respect to the context of utterance (indexicality), meanings that place requirements on what language users take for granted (presuppositions), and meanings that are the result of predictable contextual inferences (implicatures). The focus is on empirical issues.

name: Syntax 1
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 201.
description: Introduction to the study of generative syntax of natural languages, emphasizing basic concepts and formalism: phrase structure rules, transformations, and conditions on rules.

name: Structure of a Specific Language 1
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: LING 330 and LING 331 and LING 371, or permission of instructor.
description: Application and refinement of analytical methods in phonology, morphology, and syntax to phenomena from a specific language. One focus will be the identification of empirical generalizations which form the basis for the development of the theory. The language of study will vary from year to year.

name: Structure of an Indigenous Language
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: LING 331 and LING 371 or permission of instructor.
description:
Investigates linguistic and grammatical properties in an Indigenous language or language family of North America. Topics covered include: phonetics, phonology,
morphology, syntax, and semantics; historical and comparative research; as well as topics at the intersection of language and culture. The course engages with primary literature on the language of study.

name: Field Methods of Linguistics
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: LING 330, LING 331 and LING 371.
description: Elicitation, recording and analysis of linguistic data under simulated field conditions; consideration of typical problems confronting the field analyst, preparation of a descriptive statement.

name: Topics at the Interfaces 1
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: LING 360 and LING 371 and permission of instructor.
description: Topics relevant to a linguistic interface, rotating between syntax/semantics interface and morphology/syntax interface.

name: Topics at the Interfaces 2
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: LING 331, LING 371 or permission of instructor.
description: Topics relevant to a linguistic interface, rotating between phonology/syntax interface and morphology/phonology interface.

name: Linguistic Theory and  its Foundations
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: Two of LING 331, LING 360, LING 371, LING 440.
description: Change linguistics underwent at the end of the 1950's both in how it conceived of itself and in the methods it used, including the philosophical change and the formal and mathematical innovations in syntax and morphology.

name: Historical Linguistics
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: LING 330 and LING 320 or permission of instructor.
description: An examination of how languages change over time and the methods that allow us to study linguistic history. Topics include: types of language change (sound change, anology, etc.) linguistic reconstruction, the origins of modern languages.

name: Morphology
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 330 and LING 371, or permission of the instructor
description: An introduction to the study of the internal structure of words. Topics will include the different ways words are formed in languages, how sound changes take place within words, how words are used in sentences.

name:
Computational
 Linguistics
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): COMP 250 and MATH 133 or permission of the instructor.
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken or are taking COMP 445.
description:
Introduction to foundational ideas in computational linguistics and natural language
processing. Topics include formal language theory, probability theory, estimation and inference, and recursively defined models of language structure. Emphasis on both the mathematical foundations of the field as well as how to use these tools to understand human language.

name: Laboratory Linguistics
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: LING 201 and either LING 330 or LING 331, or permission of instructor.
description: Students with a background in some core area(s) of linguistics will learn how to test linguistic theories in the lab. The focus is on learning by doing: Students will design and carry out their own experiments, and will learn some basic statistics to evaluate them.

name: Acquisition of Phonology
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 331; a course in language acquisition highly recommended.
description: Exploration of the development of prosodic and segmental structure in children, with an emphasis on current theoretically-informed work in this area.

name: Semantics 2
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 360
description: This course elaborates on the rudiments of natural language semantics developed in LING 360.

name: Formal Methods in Linguistics
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: LING 360 or PHIL 210 or permission of instructor.
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken LING 560 or MATH 240.
description: This course presents the formal methods used in the study of language (namely, the theories of sets, relations, functions, partial orders, and lattices, as well as the principle of mathematical induction).

name: Pragmatics 2
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): LING 360, LING 365, PHIL 210 or permission of the instructor.
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken LING 565.
description: Advanced study of contemporary formal theories about the relationship between language and its contexts of use. Topics include deixis, presupposition and implicature.

name: Honours Thesis
subject: LING
description: Honours thesis.

name: Honours Thesis
subject: LING
description: Honours thesis.

name: Honours Thesis
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 480D1.
description: See LING 480D1 for course description.

name: Honours Thesis
subject: LING
description: Honours thesis.

name: Honours Thesis
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 480N1
description: See LING 480N1 for course description.

name: Joint Honours Thesis
subject: LING
description: Research and writing of the Joint Honours thesis on an approved subject.

name: Joint Honours Thesis
subject: LING
description: Research and writing of the Joint Honours thesis on an approved subject.

name: Joint Honours Thesis
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 481D1
description: Research and writing of the Joint Honours thesis on an approved subject.

name: Special Topics 1
subject: LING
restrictions: Permission of instructor.
description: Intensive study of a selected field or topic.

name: Special Topics 2
subject: LING
restrictions: Permission of instructor.
description: Intensive study of a selected field or topic.

name: Special Topics 3
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 331 or LING 370 or LING 371 or permission of instructor.
description: Intensive study of a selected field or topic.

name: Independent Study 1
subject: LING
restrictions: Permission of instructor.
description: Independent study of a selected field or topic.

name: Independent Study 2
subject: LING
restrictions: Permission of instructor.
description: Independent study of a selected field or topic.

name: Internship: Linguistics
subject: LING
restrictions: Limited to U2 and U3 students, with a minimum CGPA of 2.7, and permission of the department.
description: Internship with a host institution or organization.

name: Sociolinguistics 2
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 320 or permission of instructor.
description: A seminar on variationist "micro-sociolinguistics", including a survey of the most important primary literature on sociolinguistic variation and introduction to sociolinguistic fieldwork.

name: Dialectology
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: LING 330 and LING 320.
description: An introduction to the theory and methods of dialectology (the study of regional variation in language) with an emphasis on connections with linguistic theory. Students will also acquire a practical knowledge of major differences among dialects of English, and will gain hands-on experience in the planning, implementation and analysis of a dialect survey.

name: Acoustic Phonetics
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: LING 201 and LING 330 or permission of instructor
description: This course will introduce students to the fundamental principles of acoustic phonetics, focusing on an acoustic model of sound production by the vocal tract and the principles and techniques of acoustic analysis of speech. Classes will be a mix of lectures and hands-on lab-based activities and class discussions.

name: Phonology 2
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 331 or permission of instructor.
description: Exploration of current issues in phonology.

name: Computational Semantics with Neural Networks
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: LING 345 or COMP 345 or LING 445 or COMP 445 or COMP 551 or COMP 550 or equivalent machine learning courses
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken or are taking COMP 545.
description: Exploration of how to represent the meaning of words, phrases, sentences, and discourse using neural networks, and tying these ideas to formal semantics. Critical evaluation of existing neural networks and their resulting representations, their limitations, and investigation of new network architectures or training procedures to solve these issues.

name: Syntax 2
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 371
description: This course extends and refines the theory of grammar developed in LING 371, while introducing some primary literature and developments (in certain modules of the grammar such as phrase structure, wh-movement, and binding).

name: Graduate Research Seminar 1
subject: LING
description: A seminar dedicated to graduate student research and professional training.

name: Graduate Research Seminar 2
subject: LING
description: A seminar dedicated to graduate student research and professional training.

name: M.A. Research 1
subject: LING
description: A fundemental course in which M.A. students learn how to do independent research.

name: M.A. Research 2
subject: LING
description: An advanced course in which M.A. students conduct their own research project.

name: M.A. Research Paper
subject: LING
description: Research and writing of the M.A. research paper on an approved subject.

name: Linguistic Field Research
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: LING 631 and LING 671
description: Explores theoretically relevant questions in a less familiar and under-studied language, working with a language consultant. Basic methods of how to approach a new language and culture in the field are covered, as well as issues of language documentation and language preservation.

name: Analysis of an Indigenous Language
subject: LING
description: Engages with primary research into linguistic and  grammatical properties in an Indigenous language or language family of North America. Topics covered include: phonetics, phonology, morphology, syntax, and  semantics; historical and comparative research; as well as topics at the intersection of language and culture. Evaluates linguistic theories with respect to empirical data from an Indigenous language, and covers how Indigenous languages have shaped linguistic theory.

name: Experimental Linguistics: Methods
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: PSYC 305 or equivalent
description: Introduction to experimental research methods for linguists.

name: Phonetics 3
subject: LING
description: Introduction to the current state of the field of phonetics, covering articulation, acoustics, and theories of perception and production.

name: Phonology 3
subject: LING
description: Foundations of phonological theory, focusing on issues in segmental and prosodic structure.

name: Phonetics and Phonology 4
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite(s): LING 631 and LING 630, or permission of instructor.
description: Advanced course on current issues in phonetic and phonological theory.

name: Fundamentals of Morphology
subject: LING
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken LING 440
description: Introduction to current theoretical notions that seek to define a well-formed word structure, including headedness, morphological subcategorization, feature percolation and cyclicity.

name: Computational Research on Language
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: LING 631 and LING 671 or permission of instructor.
description: Introduction to computational tools and methods for addressing research questions in language sciences, including analysis of text and speech datasets.

name: Testing Theories in the Laboratory
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: Permission of instructor
description: A course combining linguistic theory and appropriate experimental methods. Students will work on theoretical problems and develop techniques to test them experimentally. They will learn how to design and run experiments, and to evaluate experimental results and present them in a theoretical context.

name: Topics in Acquisition of Phonology
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: LING 331 or LING 631 or permission of instructor. A course in language acquisition is highly recommended.
description: An examination of theoretically informed work on the first language acquisition of phonology.

name: Semantics 3
subject: LING
description: Introduction to the basics of natural language semantics.

name: Advanced Formal Methods
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 660 or LING 671.
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken LING 560.
description: This course presents the formal methods used in the study of syntax or semantics. Topics include basic set theory as well as partial orders and lattices.

name: Semantics 4
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 660.
description: Further introduction to the basics of natural language semantics.

name: Syntax 3
subject: LING
description: In-depth overview of current issues in theoretical syntax. Emphasis will be placed on the logic and development of argumentation in syntactic theory.

name: Syntax 4
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 671
description: Continuation of LING 671.

name: Selected Topics 1
subject: LING
description: Intensive study of a selected topic in linguistics.

name: Selected Topics 2
subject: LING
description: Intensive study of a selected topic in linguistics.

name: Tutorial 1
subject: LING
restrictions: Permission of instructor.
description: Independent study of a selected field or topic.

name: M.A. Thesis Submission
subject: LING
description: Research, writing and submission of the M.A. thesis on an approved subject.

name: Language Acquisition Issues 2
subject: LING
description: Examination and discussion of the leterature on a selected topic in language acquisition.

name: Advanced Seminar 1
subject: LING
description: An advanced seminar on current linguistic research.

name: Advanced Seminar 2
subject: LING
description: An advanced seminar on current linguistic research.

name: Seminar in Phonology
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 635
description: Current topics in phonological theory.

name: Seminar in Semantics
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 665
description: Current topics in semantic theory.

name: Seminar in Syntax
subject: LING
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: LING 671
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken LING 771
description: Exploration and in-depth discussion of a current topic in syntactic theory through reading and discussion of primary literature.

name: Selected Topics 3
subject: LING
description: Intensive study of a selected topic in linguistics

name: Selected Topics 4
subject: LING
description: Intensive study of selected topic in linguistics.

name: Tutorial 2
subject: LING
restrictions: Permission of instructor.
description: Independent study of a selected field or topic.

name: Tutorial 3
subject: LING
restrictions: Permission of instructor.
description: Independent study of a selected field or topic.

name: Mathematics for Education Students
subject: MATH
restrictions: Open only to students in the B.Ed. program, not open to students who have successfully completed CEGEP course 201-101 or an equivalent. Not available for credit with MATH 112
description: Sets and functions. Numeration systems. Whole numbers and integers, algorithms for whole-number computations, elementary number theory. Fractions and proportional reasoning. Real numbers, decimals and percents. A brief introduction to probability and statistics.

name: Calculus for Management
subject: MATH
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: A course in functions.
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken or are taking MATH 139 or MATH 140 or MATH 150 or CEGEP objective 00UN. MATH 139, MATH 140, MATH 141, MATH 150 , and MATH 151 are not open to students who have taken or are taking MATH 122, except by special permission of the Department of Mathematics and Statistics. Open to Desautels Faculty of Management students only.
description: Review of functions, exponents and radicals,exponential and logarithm. Examples of functions in business applications. Limits, continuity and derivatives. Differentiation of elementary functions. Antiderivatives. The definite integral. Techniques of Integration. Applications of differentiation and integration.Trigonometric functions are not discussed in this course.

name: Linear Algebra and Probability
subject: MATH
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken or are taking MATH 133 or CEGEP objective 00UQ or equivalent.
description: Geometric vectors in low dimensions. Lines and planes. Dot and cross product. Linear equations and matrices. Matrix operations, properties and rank. Linear dependence and independence. Inverses and determinants. Linear programming and tableaux. Sample space, probability, combination of events. Conditional probability and Bayes Law. Random sampling. Random variables and common distributions.

name: Mathematical Techniques for Economics
subject: MATH
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken MATH 122 or MATH 141 or CEGEP objective 00UP or an equivalent CEGEP course covering integral calculus.
description: Differentation of single and multivarible functions; optimization of single and multivarible functions; constrained optimization of multivariable functions; elements of integration; applications to economics.

name: Linear Algebra and Geometry
subject: MATH
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: a course in functions
restrictions: 1) Not open to students who have taken CEGEP objective 00UQ or equivalent. 2) Not open to students who have taken or are taking MATH 123, except by permission of the Department of Mathematics and Statistics.
description: Systems of linear equations, matrices, inverses, determinants; geometric vectors in three dimensions, dot product, cross product, lines and planes; introduction to vector spaces, linear dependence and independence, bases. Linear transformations. Eigenvalues and diagonalization.

name: Calculus 1 with Precalculus
subject: MATH
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: a course in functions
restrictions: 1) Not open to students who have taken CEGEP objective 00UN or equivalent.2) Not open to students who have taken or are taking MATH 140 or MATH 150. 3) Not open to students who have taken or are taking MATH 122, except by permission of the Department of Mathematics and Statistics.
description: Review of trigonometry and other Precalculus topics. Limits, continuity, derivative. Differentiation of elementary functions. Antidifferentiation. Applications.

name: Calculus 1
subject: MATH
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: High School Calculus
restrictions: 1) Not open to students who have taken MATH139 or MATH 150 or CEGEP objective 00UN or equivalent. 2) Not open to students who have taken or are taking MATH 122, except by permission of the Department of Mathematics and Statistics.
description: Review of functions and graphs. Limits, continuity, derivative. Differentiation of elementary functions. Antidifferentiation. Applications.

name: Calculus 2
subject: MATH
prerequisitesText: Prerequisites: MATH 139 or MATH 140 or MATH 150.
restrictions: Not open to students who have taken or are taking MATH 122,except by permission of the Department of Mathematics and Statistics.
description: The definite integral. Techniques of integration. Applications. Introduction to sequences and series.

name: Calculus A
subject: MATH
restrictions: 1) Not open to students who have taken or are taking MATH 139 or MATH 140 or CEGEP objective 00UN or equivalent. 2) Not open to students who have taken or are taking MATH 122,except by permission of the Department of Mathematics and Statistics.
description: Functions, limits and continuity, differentiation, L'Hospital's rule, applications, Taylor polynomials, parametric curves, functions of several variables.

name: Calculus B
subject: MATH
prerequisitesText: Prerequisite: MATH 150
